world.title: Latvia merges gambling watchdog with tax authority in regulatory restructure
world.description: Latvia has announced a regulatory restructure, with Lottery and Gambling Supervision Inspectorate integrating into the State Revenue Service.

world.title: United Nigeria Airlines Admitted to IATA Interline Agreement, Opening Path to Global Partnerships
world.description: United Nigeria Airlines (UNA) has been admitted to the International Air Transport Association's (IATA) Multilateral Interline Traffic Agreement (MITA) for passenger operations, marking a significant step in the airline's transition from a domestic carrier to a globally connected operator.

world.title: Namibia Launches $23M Financing Facility for Women-Owned Businesses
world.description: Namibia has launched DBN For Her, a dedicated financing facility for 100% women-owned enterprises, backed by the African Development Bank (AfDB) through its Affirmative Finance Action for Women in Africa (AFAWA) programme.

world.title: SANParks Honorary Rangers Target R1.5 Million Through Comrades Marathon Charity Campaign
world.description: The KwaZulu-Natal Region of the SANParks Honorary Rangers (SHR) is seeking to raise R1.5 million through the Comrades Marathon Association's Amabeadibeadi Race4Charity campaign, matching the amount raised through the same initiative in 2025.

world.title: 'Soft power' is a buzzword these days. What is it exactly? : NPR
world.description: The U.S. government long saw giving international aid as a way to build goodwill throughout the world. Did it work? And what does the reducing of foreign aid mean for that effort now?

world.title: China remains the leading international trading partner with the East African Community
world.description: Trade in the East African Community (EAC) rose to US$ 42.4 billion as the region sustained the steady expansion observed throughout the year.

world.title: World Bank guarantee arm shifts to portfolio approach as hits $100 billion milestone
world.description: LONDON, April 9 (Reuters) – The World Bank Group’s Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA) has surpassed $100 billion in total guarantee issuance since its founding almost 40 years ago, reaching the milestone after backing a raft of renewable power and battery storage projects, the agency said on Thursday. business.title: Workplace sexual harassment complaints jump by 38% in Hong Kong
business.description: Equal Opportunities Commission recorded 315 such complaints in 2025, up from 228 in previous year.

business.title: Analyst says Metro Vancouver gas price to drop 16 cents per litre overnight

business.description: Cruising has grown rapidly, driven by rising travel demand, unique experiential offerings, and strong value appeal in today’s economy.

business.description: Lenovo announces it has completed the acquisition of Infinidat, a global provider of high-end enterprise storage solutions.

business.title: Hong Kong seeks to raise bond issuance ceiling to HK$900 billion
business.description: Government proposes to raise borrowing ceiling from HK$700 billion to HK$900 billion to fund infrastructure development.

business.title: American Airlines reveals higher bag fees, new basic economy restrictions
business.description: American Airlines became the latest to hike checked bag fees as the industry grapples with rising prices. The carrier also tightened its basic economy policies.
